topic: Two colleagues are both considered for the same promotion
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the office hummed with a grating monotony that seemed to vibrate directly behind my eyes. I watched Sarah casually accept the congratulations from Mr. Henderson, her smile bright and effortless. My jaw tightened almost imperceptibly. I rearranged the papers on my desk with unnecessary precision, the crisp sound of the movement echoing in the sudden quiet of my focus. *Of course*, I thought, a bitter taste rising in my throat. I picked up my lukewarm coffee, the ceramic mug feeling suddenly heavy and cold in my hand. This was just another Tuesday, another parade of undeserved accolades for someone else.

[story 2]

"Congratulations again, Sarah," I managed, my voice sounding unnaturally level as I clapped my hands together once, sharply. The sound seemed too loud in the open-plan workspace. I didn't look up from the spreadsheet I was ostensibly reviewing, focusing intently on a column of figures that offered no comfort. Every time someone mentioned the upcoming leadership role, a distinct pressure built behind my sternum, making my shoulders tense up beneath my blazer. I shifted in my chair, the cheap plastic groaning in protest, wishing the floor would simply swallow me whole.

[story 3]

I leaned back in my chair, letting out a slow, controlled breath that barely disturbed the air around me. The silence in the breakroom felt thick, suffocating. I picked at a loose thread on my sleeve, my gaze fixed on the water cooler, refusing to meet anyone’s eyes. When Mark started recounting the details of Sarah’s recent success, I nodded too quickly, my expression carefully neutral. Internally, a distinct knot formed in my stomach, a tight, unpleasant coil that refused to loosen, demanding a physical reaction I couldn't quite summon.
